explanation: Appropriation account is the account into which the net profit found in the profit and loss account of the partner will be carried down to and shared according to their sharing ratio

explanation: It is an expense incurred in order to enhance the sales of the company's product or services

explanation: Call in arrears is the amount called by the company which is not paid by the shareholders before the due date fixed for payment

subject: accounting
text: Show how the following transaction will be recorded applying the double-entry principle: Rent N50,000 was paid by Mr. Roi to his landlord on 1st July by cheque.

options.A: Dr Rent A/c; Dr Bank A/c
options.B: Dr Bank A/c; Cr Rent A/c
options.C: Dr Rent A/c; Cr Bank A/c
options.D: Dr Rent A/c; Cr Mr. Roi

explanation: Rent account is debited and bank account is credited (money has gone out via the cheque)

explanation: Personal accounts are for individuals (debtors, creditors, banks, capital, drawings); impersonal accounts are not held in the name of persons

text: Goods were purchased for resale on credit costing N150,000 on 30th September from Tosanwumi International. The entry to record this transaction is debit

options.C: Credit Account N150,000, Credit Tosanwumi International N150,000
options.D: Tosanwumi International N150,000, credit credit Account N150,000

explanation: The purchase account is debited while the Tosanwumi International account is credited

explanation: A treasury receipt serves as proof of payment made to the government and is an official record of the transaction

explanation: A partnership includes an Appropriation Account showing how profit is distributed among partners

explanation: The income and expenditure account in a non-profit organization serves the same function as the profit and loss account in a for-profit organization

text: Salaries in arrears is treated in the balance sheet as a
explanation: Salaries in arrears are amounts owed to employees expected to be paid in the near future, so they are a current liability

explanation: The pay-in-slip is used to deposit money into a bank account and serves as proof of the transaction

explanation: A provisional general warrant authorises spending before the approval of the appropriate bill at the beginning of the year

subject: accounting
text: Goodwill can be introduced when
explanation: Goodwill can be introduced when a new partner is admitted, as it changes the partnership's value and earning capacity

subject: accounting
text: The amount by which assets exceed liabilities is
explanation: From Assets = Liabilities + Owner's Equity, the difference between assets and liabilities represents capital

subject: accounting
text: Where there is no partnership agreement, a partner who advances a loan to the partnership is entitled to ____ interest.
explanation: In the absence of a partnership agreement, the legally recognized interest rate on a partner's loan is 5% per annum

subject: accounting
text: The following balances were extracted from the books of Onuoha, a trader, on 31st December 2005: Audit fee 12000; General expenses 30000; Purchases 70000; Commission paid 30000; Stock (01-01-2005) 10000; Stock (31-12-2005) 15000; Sales 120000. The gross profit is
explanation: COGS = Opening Stock + Purchases - Closing Stock = 10,000 + 70,000 - 15,000 = 65,000; Gross Profit = Sales - COGS = 120,000 - 65,000 = 55,000

subject: accounting
text: Depreciation is

options.C: loss in the value of fixed asset

explanation: Depreciation is the reduction in the value of a fixed asset due to wear and tear, usage, obsolescence or the passage of time
